article_title: IBM, Red Hat, and Google Donate Kubernetes LLM Inference Framework llm-d to the CNCF

content_preview.title: IBM, Red Hat, and Google Donate Kubernetes LLM Inference Framework llm-d to the CNCF
content_preview.summary: IBM Research, Red Hat, and Google Cloud donated llm-d, a Kubernetes-native LLM inference framework, to the CNCF as a sandbox project at KubeCon Europe 2026, backed by NVIDIA, CoreWeave, AMD, and other partners.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

IBM Research, Red Hat, and Google Cloud announced the donation of llm-d, an open-source distributed inference framework for large language models, to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation as a sandbox project. The announcement was made on March 24, 2026, at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe in Amsterdam, according to the [CNCF blog](https://www.cncf.io/blog/2026/03/24/welcome-llm-d-to-the-cncf-evolving-kubernetes-into-sota-ai-infrastructure/).

The project, originally launched in May ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong technical coverage of a significant cloud-native AI infrastructure donation. Good detail on architecture (prefill/decode disaggregation, KV-cache offloading, Gateway API).
editor_notes.source_verification: CNCF blog (fetched): Confirms Sandbox acceptance, 120k tokens/sec on 16 H100s, founding coalition (Red Hat, Google Cloud, IBM, CoreWeave, NVIDIA), prefill/decode disaggregation, KV-cache management, Gateway API integration. IBM Research (fetched): Confirms Carlos Costa quote, technical architecture details, multi-accelerator support, CNCF donation rationale. SiliconAngle (fetched): Confirms KubeCon EU 2026, Brian Stevens/Robert Shaw, disaggregated serving, enterprise focus.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. 120k tokens/sec benchmark, founding coalition, and technical architecture all confirmed across sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Technical depth appropriate for developer audience.
editor_notes.originality: No prior llm-d coverage. Distinct from NVIDIA GPU DRA driver article (different KubeCon announcement).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-sourced cloud infrastructure news. Ready for publication.
